mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lifts equity valuations by lowering the discount rate and easing financial conditions, so subsequent S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive when policy is still moving down.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is declining on a 3-month basis, or the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income, which reduces expected earnings and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's rolling 3-year hit rate is indistinguishable from 50% at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rebound in RSAFS and HOUST indicates improving final demand and housing-cycle transmission, which supports near-term earnings expectations and pushes the S&P 500 higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-signal state does not beat each single-series effect: the combined RSAFS-HOUST condition must improve 60-day forward returns by at least 1 percentage point versus either proxy alone over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO series signals industrial output recovery and stronger realized corporate activity, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month rising INDPRO episodes fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time, or if average forward return is not above unconditional baseline by at least 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is falling while UNRATE is rising, the labor market is deteriorating in a way that directly weakens demand and earnings, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition PAYEMS down and UNRATE up does not outperform either component alone in predicting negative 20-day returns, or if the conditional hit rate is below 55% over any 2-year span.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level after a rate-hike cycle compresses valuation multiples and tightens credit, so the S&P 500 tends to underperform over the next quarter when policy stays restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-rate episodes above 4.5% do not coincide with below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the median forward return is not negative across a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad demand acceleration across output and consumption, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through stronger earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to raise 20-day forward returns above unconditional baseline by at least 0.5 percentage points, or if the sign accuracy falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment, a falling CPIAUCSL combined with stable or easier FEDFUNDS policy increases real liquidity and lowers discount rates, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL declines on a 3-month annualized basis
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes do not outperform neutral inflation periods for 5-day forward returns, or if positive returns are not observed in at least 58% of triggered instances.
